viewer_promise: The video shows how a Hyatt Regency walkway drawing change doubled a critical load and how the review process accepted the changed load path.
recommended_title: Hyatt Regency: The Drawing Change That Doubled the Load | Cascade Effects

alternate_titles.tradeoff: Slightly tighter and more direct, but less searchable for the exact hotel name.

alternate_titles.title: Hyatt Regency Walkway Collapse: The Load Path Change | Cascade Effects
The Walkway Failure Hidden in a Drawing Change | Cascade Effects
Hyatt Regency: When a Detail Changed the Structure | Cascade Effects
alternate_titles.tradeoff: Good mechanism framing, less explicit about doubled load.

description:
On July 17, 1981, a tea dance filled the Kansas City Hyatt Regency atrium while suspended walkways crossed overhead. This video follows the drawing change that doubled the load at a critical hanger-rod connection and shows how an orderly approval process accepted the wrong load path.

The story is not just that a connection failed. It is how a structural change moved through drawings, assumptions, reviews, and warnings until the system treated a changed load path like paperwork.

Chapters:
00:00 The Tea Dance
01:32 The Original Load Path
03:11 The Revision
05:08 The Category Error
08:05 The Warning Signs
12:43 Everything Was In Order

Sources and references:
NIST/NBS Building Science Series 143: Investigation of the Kansas City Hyatt Regency Walkways Collapse
https://doi.org/10.6028/NBS.BSS.143

NIST: Walkway Collapse, Kansas City, Missouri, 1981
https://www.nist.gov/el/walkway-collapse-kansas-city-missouri-1981

ASCE Civil Engineering Source: The Hyatt Regency Walkway Collapse
https://www.asce.org/publications-and-news/civil-engineering-source/civil-engineering-magazine/article/2007/01/the-hyatt-regency-walkway-collapse/
